figures.caption: Table 4: Average peak GPU memory (GB, lower is better) during inference. Avg is the unweighted mean over the four datasets; best (lowest) Avg per backbone is in bold. Peak memory is measured over the full run and may not correlate with TTFT.
Table 3: Average TTFT (ms, lower is better) on HotpotQA, NarrativeQA, QASPER, and OpenROAD. Avg is the unweighted mean over the four datasets; best (lowest) Avg per backbone is in bold.

Table 2: Rouge-L score (%, higher is better) on HotpotQA, NarrativeQA, QASPER, and OpenROAD. Best per backbone and dataset is in bold. Avg is the unweighted mean over the four datasets; best Avg per backbone is in bold.

Figure 1: Overview of H2MT (H2MT). (1) Semantic hierarchy generation (offline): convert each document into a rooted tree of semantically coherent units. (2) Hierarchical memory token construction (offline): compute leaf memories using learnable write/read embeddings, then propagate information bottom-up by aggregating children memories to form intermediate-node memories. (3) Hierarchical memory-aware inference (online): embed the query, perform top-down routing with per-parent pruning to select relevant nodes, and condition the backbone on the retrieved memory tokens to generate the answer.
Figure 3: Effect of routing𝑘 on latency andmemory (Qwen2.514B-Instruct, NarrativeQA).
Table 10: ROUGE-L (%) on the LVS dataset (backbone: Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct).
